100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On July 11, 1926, the world was buzzing with significant developments. In the United States, the ongoing debate over Prohibition intensified, with calls for reform gaining traction amidst rising public discontent. Across the Atlantic, British coal miners continued their strike, demanding better wages and working conditions, which threatened to escalate into a national crisis. Meanwhile, in Germany, political tensions mounted as the Weimar Republic grappled with economic instability and the repercussions of the Treaty of Versailles.
